title: US Phone Plan Guide
summary: How to choose a carrier, get a SIM card, and pick the right plan after arriving in the US.

content.text: A phone is one of the most essential tools after arriving in the US. With a US phone number, you can receive verification codes, use navigation, and contact your school and new friends.

items.label: Prepaid
items.description: No credit check needed, ready to use immediately. Great for new arrivals. $30-50/month.

items.label: Postpaid
items.description: Requires SSN or credit check. Better signal and service, but requires a contract. $40-70/month.

content.text: We recommend starting with a prepaid plan, then switching to postpaid once you have an SSN and credit history.

items.label: T-Mobile
items.description: Good coverage, international roaming packages, some plans include free calls to China. Top recommendation.

items.label: AT&T
items.description: Wide coverage, reliable signal. Prepaid brand Cricket is great value.

items.label: Mint Mobile
items.description: Uses T-Mobile network, cheapest option (from $15/month). Must buy 3-12 months upfront.

items.label: China Telecom Americas/CMLink
items.description: Dual US-China number, keeps your Chinese number active. Great for receiving Chinese verification codes.

items.title: Choose carrier and plan
items.description: Pick based on your budget and needs.

items.title: Visit a store or buy online
items.description: Bring your passport to a carrier store to buy a SIM card. You can also order online.

items.title: Install the SIM card
items.description: Insert the new SIM card into your phone and follow activation instructions.

items.title: Set up your phone
items.description: Download essential apps: Google Maps, WhatsApp, your school's app, etc.

content.text: If your phone is from China, make sure it supports US carrier bands. Most recent phones do, but check on the carrier's website beforehand.
